title: Brief Overview of Remote
pros:
All remote workers are 100% remote. In an admin role, you will never be asked to come into the office. There is no geographical requirement from what I understand, the only requirement (aside from having satisfactory experience) is a good internet connection. 

The hours are strict which is good and bad. If you are hired for an 8:30-5PM shift....you will work those hours. Aside from requested flexibility, you can ensure that you will start and end at the times you were hired for.
cons:
There is occasional reorganization in the departments and each person job duties. 

Not many holidays off.

title: Rewarding Place To Work
pros:
Opportunities for growth
Offers benefits
Flexible schedules with consistent hours
Great work environment
Vacation/Sick time
Yearly performance reviews
Remote work options

From my experience working here, as long as you show you're committed and have work ethic, there's always room for growth.  It's possible to start low as an office admin up to management.
cons: I don't have much to complain about, sometimes management can be direct, and expect prompt responses, but that's due to the fast paced environment.
advice_to_management: ABCO Facility Maintenance is a great place to work. I greatly appreciate the opportunities for growth offered here with the chance to learn new things. Your hard work feels appreciated.

title: Remote Flexibility Overshadowed by Poor Management and Unstable Practices
pros:
Fully remote work allows flexibility and eliminates commuting costs.

A chance to work independently and develop problem-solving skills.
cons:
-Lack of transparency regarding benefits, including vacation time.
-Poor management support; requests for guidance, SOPs, or feedback often go unanswered.
-Job security is unstable; employees can be let go without valid reason or warning.
-Limited opportunities for professional growth or advancement.
-Evasive communication and unprofessional handling of employee concerns.
-Unfair practices in handling earned benefits after layoffs.
-A culture that prioritizes profit over employee well-being and support.
advice_to_management:
-Be transparent and consistent about employee benefits and ensure employees receive what they’ve earned.
-Provide proper training, guidance, and resources to help employees succeed in their roles.
-Implement clear SOPs and policies to reduce confusion and frustration.
-Create a supportive environment that values employee contributions and fosters growth.
-Develop a structured promotion and review process to retain top talent and boost morale.
-Treat employees as valuable assets, not as disposable resources.

title: Work/Life Balance for Problem Solvers
pros:
I love the ability to work from anywhere and ABCO offers me that benefit!  Many positions were available where I could work from home.  Training when I started needed improvement but if you are independent and good at teaching yourself how to do things/finding answers on your own, you will be just fine.  Being in the construction industry, the amount of work varies day to day but it is a great work/life balance.  

If you are looking for a company that allows you use your independence and to work from home, consider working for ABCO.
cons:
I love the ability to work from anywh  Training when I started needed improvement.  The largest improvement I would like to see is the ability to get more compensation and acknowledgement of the work you do.  I was told I would have a review after the first month and that was never done.  It feels at times that the work I do is never really known.  However, I have taken no news as good news of now.  Communication about what benefits you have is also lacking.  You have it documented and need to know for yourself because you are never notified when certain benefits (other than the ability to be on the high price healthcare plan) become available to you.  

If you are looking for a company that allows you use your independence and to work from home, consider working for ABCO.
advice_to_management: There are several really good people that work for you.  Acknowledge, communicate and appreciate them!

title: Wonderful company to work for! I love my Job!
pros:
-Work from home opportunities available.  I work from home and couldn't be happier!
-Flexible schedules as well a part time. 
-Training is hands on and extensive. Training remotely was a little different but not impossible.
-Fast paced environment. Every day is a new adventure but not overwhelming. 
-Management is very approachable. 
-Paid vacation after a year. 
-Benefits are fair.
-Pay is fair and competitive.
-ABCO cares about its customers and community.  
-ABCO organizes fund raisers to help charitable causes that even remote employees can participate in.
-ABCO cares about their employees.  I have found the company to be very supportive and understanding.
cons: -Although ABCO has fair benefits offered, improvements could be made.

title: Couldn’t  Make This Up - Discoraging & Disgusting
pros: Received pay check on time
cons: During my time at this company I had seen many things not ever seen before in my past. Upper management was making sexual comments daily. Allegations of sexting was open conversation at breaks. Fraternization was taking place on the property parking lot with multiple employees. People were crying in the office. Fighting was happening at office. Stretching the truth was happening when responding to clients. Overcharging clients for material was normal. Being treated less then was part of the daily routine. Besides all of these factors manipulation was the mantra.
advice_to_management: Notice the problems. I don’t believe your bad, but you are doing bad things. Have some respect for people and dignity. Don’t ignore sexual misconduct from your leadership. Everyone knows no matter how much you sweep it up under the rug. Be true to your client, don’t make up stories for bad tradesmenship. Don’t have people falsely pass information to customers. When people are crying in an office over sexual conduct of others, or even better laughing about it. You have an issue.  I couldn’t recommend your business practices to anyone that I truly care about. Would you want your children to work at your establishment? If your answer is yes , shame on you!
